verses.text: From the fish's belly Jonah prays: 'I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the LORD, and he heard me.' Prayer in the deep. There is no place so low that God cannot hear.
From the fish's belly Jonah prays: 'I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the LORD, and he heard me.' Prayer in the deep. There is no place so low that God cannot hear.
He recounts being cast into the deep, the floods and weeds about him, banished from God's sight. Sinking despair. He felt utterly cut off, yet still he prayed.
He recounts being cast into the deep, the floods and weeds about him, banished from God's sight. Sinking despair. He felt utterly cut off, yet still he prayed.
He recounts being cast into the deep, the floods and weeds about him, banished from God's sight. Sinking despair. He felt utterly cut off, yet still he prayed.
He went down to the mountains' roots, yet God 'brought up my life from corruption'; his prayer reached the holy temple. Lifted from death. God hears and raises the soul that cries from the depths.
He went down to the mountains' roots, yet God 'brought up my life from corruption'; his prayer reached the holy temple. Lifted from death. God hears and raises the soul that cries from the depths.

'They that observe lying vanities forsake their own mercy'; salvation is of the LORD; and the fish casts Jonah on dry land. Mercy confessed. Deliverance comes when the heart owns that salvation is God's alone.
